value.found_quote: Sandboxing provides kernel-level isolation for terminal commands executed by the Agent. When enabled, commands run in a restricted environment with limited file system and network access, protecting your system from unintended modifications.
value.rationale: The page has a dedicated "Terminal Sandboxing" section describing kernel-level isolation for terminal commands executed by the Agent, running in a restricted environment with permission-governed controls (toggles, strict mode). This matches the claim. It is described as a current, configurable feature (though disabled by default), supported on macOS (Seatbelt) and Linux (nsjail), indicating active availability.
